heading: Prohibition on private funding for interstate deployment
label: 329 Prohibition on private funding for interstate deployment
text: A member of the National Guard may not be ordered to cross a border of a State to perform duty (under this title or title 10) if such duty is paid for with private funds, unless such duty is in response to a major disaster or emergency under section 401 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ().42 U.S.C. 5170
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 329, , , which related to summary courts-martial of National Guard not in Federal service, was repealed by , , .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 608Pub. L. 107–314, div. A, title V, § 512(c)(1)116 Stat. 2537Dec. 2, 2002
